title: Starbucks Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend
text: SEATTLE--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ: SBUX) today announced that its Board of Directors has approved a dividend of $0.49 per share of outstanding Common Stock. The dividend will be payable in cash on August 26, 2022 to shareholders of record on August 12, 2022. About Starbucks Since 1971, Starbucks Coffee Company has been committed to ethically sourcing and roasting high-quality arabica coffee. Today, with more than 34,000 stores worldwide, the company is the premier roaster and retailer of specialty coffee in the world. Through our unwavering commitment to excellence and our guiding principles, we bring the unique Starbucks Experience to life for every customer through every cup. To share in the experience, please visit us in our stores or online at http://news.starbucks.com or www.starbucks.com.

title: Starbucks to Host 2022 Investor Day
text: SEATTLE--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ: SBUX) will host its 2022 Investor Day in Seattle on Tuesday, September 13, 2022. The event will feature presentations, as well as a question and answer session with the company's senior leadership team members, including Howard Schultz, interim chief executive officer, and Rachel Ruggeri, chief financial officer. The event is scheduled to begin at 7:30 a.m. PT/10:30 a.m. ET and expected to conclude at approximately 3:00 p.m. PT/6:00 p.m.

title: Starbucks to Host 2022 Investor Day
text: SEATTLE--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ: SBUX) will host its 2022 Investor Day in Seattle on Tuesday, September 13, 2022. The event will feature presentations, as well as a question and answer session with the company’s senior leadership team members, including Howard Schultz, interim chief executive officer, and Rachel Ruggeri, chief financial officer. The event is scheduled to begin at 7:30 a.m. PT/10:30 a.m. ET and expected to conclude at approximately 3:00 p.m. PT/6:00 p.m. ET. Due to limited capacity, in-person attendance is by invitation only. The event will be webcast and all interested parties are invited to access the webcast from the Events & Presentations section of the company’s website. A replay of the webcast and slides shown during the presentations will be available on the company’s website the following day. About Starbucks Since 1971, Starbucks Coffee Company has been committed to ethically sourcing and roasting high-quality arabica coffee. Today, with more than 34,000 stores worldwide, the company is the premier roaster and retailer of specialty coffee in the world. Through our unwavering commitment to excellence and our guiding principles, we bring the unique Starbucks Experience to life for every customer through every cup. To share in the experience, please visit us in our stores or online at http://news.starbucks.com or www.starbucks.com. Forward-Looking Statements Certain statements contained herein are “forward-looking” statements within the meaning of the applicable securities laws and regulations. Generally, these statements can be identified by the use of words such as “aim,” “anticipate,” “believe,” “continue,” “could,” “estimate,” “expect,” “feel,” “forecast,” “intend,” “may,” “outlook,” “plan,” “potential,” “project,” “seek,” “should,” “will,” “would,” and similar expressions intended to identify forward-looking statements, although not all forward-looking statements contain these identifying words. These statements include statements relating to trends in or expectations relating to the effects of our existing and any future initiatives, strategies, investments and plans, as well as trends in or expectations regarding our financial results and long-term growth model and drivers; our operations in the U.S. and China; our environmental, social and governance efforts; our partners; economic and consumer trends, including the impact of inflationary pressures; the conversion of several market operations to fully licensed models; our plans for streamlining our operations, including store openings, closures and changes in store formats and models; expanding our licensing to Nestlé of our consumer packaged goods and Foodservice businesses and its effects on our Channel Development segment results; tax rates; business opportunities and expansion; strategic acquisitions; our dividends programs; commodity costs and our mitigation strategy; our liquidity, cash flow from operations, investments, borrowing capacity and use of proceeds; continuing compliance with our covenants under our credit facilities and commercial paper program; repatriation of cash to the U.S.; the likelihood of the issuance of additional debt and the applicable interest rate; the continuing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our financial results and future availability of governmental subsidies for COVID-19 or other public health events; our ceo transition; our share repurchase program; our use of cash and cash requirements; the expected effects of new accounting pronouncements and the estimated impact of changes in U.S. tax law, including on tax rates, investments funded by these changes and potential outcomes; and effects of legal proceedings. Such statements are based on currently available operating, financial and competitive information and are subject to various risks and uncertainties. Actual future results and trends may differ materially depending on a variety of factors, including, but not limited to: further spread of COVID-19 and related disruptions to our business; regulatory measures or voluntary actions that may be put in place to limit the spread of COVID-19, including restrictions on business operations or social distancing requirements, and the duration and efficacy of such restrictions; the resurgence of COVID-19 infections and the circulation of novel variants of COVID-19; fluctuations in U.S. and international economies and currencies; our ability to preserve, grow and leverage our brands; the ability of our business partners and third-party providers to fulfill their responsibilities and commitments; potential negative effects of incidents involving food or beverage-borne illnesses, tampering, adulteration, contamination or mislabeling; potential negative effects of material breaches of our information technology systems to the extent we experience a material breach; material failures of our information technology systems; costs associated with, and the successful execution of, the Company’s initiatives and plans; new initiatives and plans or revisions to existing initiatives or plans; our ability to obtain financing on acceptable terms; the acceptance of the Company’s products by our customers, evolving consumer preferences and tastes and changes in consumer spending behavior; partner investments, changes in the availability and cost of labor including any union organizing efforts and our responses to such efforts; failure to attract or retain key executive or employee talent; significant increased logistics costs; inflationary pressures; the impact of competition; inherent risks of operating a global business including any potential negative effects stemming from the Russian invasion of Ukraine; the prices and availability of coffee, dairy and other raw materials; the effect of legal proceedings; the effects of changes in tax laws and related guidance and regulations that may be implemented and other risks detailed in the Company’s filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including in the “Risk Factors” and “Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations” sections of the Company's most recently filed periodic reports on Form 10-K and Form 10-Q and subsequent filings. The company assumes no obligation to update any of these forward-looking statements.

title: La Junta Directiva de Starbucks ratifica los detalles de la transición del director ejecutivo
text: SEATTLE--(BUSINESS WIRE)--La Junta Directiva de Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ: SBUX) proporcionó hoy noticias a favor de ratificar los detalles de la búsqueda del próximo director ejecutivo de la compañía. Como se indicó durante la teleconferencia para analizar los estados financieros trimestrales de la Compañía el mes pasado, la búsqueda de la Junta continúa encaminada para identificar a un director ejecutivo sucesor en los próximos meses, y la Junta acordó con Schultz que permanecerá como director ejecutivo interino hasta el primer trimestre fiscal de 2023. Este cronograma brinda a la compañía la vía ideal para una transición sin contratiempos y la continuidad del liderazgo durante la temporada navideña de 2022, a medida que continúa la transformación comercial. Desde que regresó como director ejecutivo interino el 4 de abril, Schultz se ha comprometido profundamente con los socios (empleados) en todos los niveles de la compañía, trabajando directamente con el equipo de liderazgo para dar forma a un plan estratégico hacia el futuro de una Starbucks Coffee Company reinventada. El plan de reinvención se está diseñando mediante la creación conjunta en toda la organización con un enfoque en superar las expectativas tanto de los socios como de los clientes. A medida que se nombre al próximo director ejecutivo para el puesto, él o ella se sumergirán por completo en la Compañía, colaborando directamente con Schultz. Con vistas a un anuncio este otoño, la transferencia oficial tendrá lugar en el primer trimestre calendario de 2023. A partir de ese momento, Schultz permanecerá en la Junta de Starbucks. Acerca de Starbucks Desde 1971, Starbucks Coffee Company ha estado comprometida con la ética de abastecimiento y tostado de alta calidad del café arábica. En la actualidad, con más de 34 000 tiendas en todo el mundo, la compañía es el principal tostador y minorista de café de especialidad en el mundo. A través de nuestro compromiso permanente con la excelencia y nuestros principios rectores, le damos vida a la Experiencia Starbucks única para cada cliente, en cada taza. Para compartir la experiencia, visítenos en nuestras tiendas o en línea en stories.starbucks.com o www.starbucks.com. Declaraciones a futuro Ciertas declaraciones contenidas en el presente constituyen “declaraciones a futuro” dentro del significado de las leyes y regulaciones sobre valores que sean aplicables. En general, dichas declaraciones se pueden identificar por el uso de términos tales como “aspiramos”, “anticipamos”, “creemos”, “continuamos”, “podría”, “estimamos”, “esperamos”, “sentimos”, “pronosticamos”, “pretende”, “puede”, “previsión”, “planificamos”, “potencial”, “proyectamos”, “buscamos”, “debería”, “será”, “sería” y expresiones similares usadas para manifestar declaraciones a futuro, aun cuando no todas las declaraciones a futuro contengan estas palabras identificatorias. Estas declaraciones incluyen declaraciones relacionadas con tendencias o expectativas relacionadas a los efectos de nuestras iniciativas, las estrategias, las inversiones y los planes existentes y futuros, así como las tendencias o las expectativas con respecto a nuestros resultados financieros y el modelo e impulsores de crecimiento a largo plazo; nuestras operaciones en EE. UU. y China; nuestros esfuerzos ambientales, sociales y de gobernanza; nuestros socios; tendencias económicas y de consumo, incluido el impacto de las presiones inflacionarias; la conversión de varias operaciones de mercado a modelos con licencia completa; nuestros planes de optimizar nuestras operaciones, incluidas las aperturas y los cierres de tiendas y los cambios en los formatos y modelos de las tiendas; expandir nuestra concesión de licencias a Nestlé de nuestros negocios de bienes de consumo empaquetados y servicio de alimentos y sus efectos en los resultados de nuestro segmento de desarrollo de canales; las tasas de impuestos; las oportunidades de negocio y expansión; las adquisiciones estratégicas; nuestros programas de dividendos; los costos de los productos básicos y nuestra estrategia de mitigación; nuestra liquidez, el flujo de caja de operaciones, inversiones, capacidad de endeudamiento y el uso de los fondos; el cumplimiento continuo de nuestros compromisos en virtud de nuestras líneas de crédito y el programa de papel comercial; repatriación de efectivo a los EE. UU.; la probabilidad de emisión de deuda adicional y la tasa de interés aplicable; el impacto continuo de la pandemia de la COVID-19 en nuestros resultados financieros y la disponibilidad futura de subsidios gubernamentales para la COVID-19 u otros eventos de salud pública; nuestra transición de director ejecutivo; nuestro programa de recompra de acciones; nuestro uso de efectivo y requerimientos de efectivo; los efectos esperados de los nuevos pronunciamientos contables y el impacto estimado de los cambios en la ley fiscal de los EE. UU., incluidas las tasas impositivas, las inversiones financiadas por estos cambios y los resultados potenciales; y los efectos de los procedimientos judiciales. Dichas declaraciones se basan en la información operativa, financiera y competitiva actualmente disponible y están sujetas a diversos riesgos e incertidumbres. Las tendencias y resultados futuros reales pueden diferir materialmente dependiendo de una variedad de factores, que incluyen, entre otros: una mayor propagación de la COVID-19 y las interrupciones relacionadas con nuestro negocio; las medidas reglamentarias o las acciones voluntarias que puedan implementarse para limitar la propagación de la COVID-19, incluidas las restricciones en las operaciones comerciales o requisitos de distanciamiento social, y la duración y eficacia de dichas restricciones; el resurgimiento de las infecciones por la COVID-19 y la circulación de nuevas variantes de la COVID-19; fluctuaciones en las economías y monedas estadounidenses e internacionales; nuestra capacidad para preservar, hacer crecer y aprovechar nuestras marcas; la capacidad de nuestros socios comerciales y de los proveedores externos de cumplir con sus responsabilidades y compromisos; los efectos negativos potenciales de incidentes relacionados con enfermedades transmitidas por alimentos o bebidas, la manipulación, la adulteración, la contaminación o el etiquetado incorrecto; los posibles efectos negativos de fallas materiales de nuestros sistemas de tecnología de la información en la medida en que experimentemos una falla material; las fallas materiales de nuestros sistemas de tecnología de la información; los costos asociados y la ejecución exitosa de las iniciativas y planes de la Compañía; las nuevas iniciativas y planes o revisiones de iniciativas o planes existentes; nuestra capacidad para obtener financiamiento en términos aceptables; la aceptación de los productos de la Compañía por parte de nuestros clientes, la evolución de las preferencias y gustos de los consumidores y los cambios en el comportamiento del gasto de los consumidores; las inversiones de los socios, los cambios en la disponibilidad y el costo de la mano de obra, incluidos los esfuerzos de organización sindical y nuestras respuestas a tales esfuerzos; la incapacidad para atraer o retener el talento clave de ejecutivos o de empleados; el aumento significativo de los costos de logística; las presiones inflacionarias; el impacto de la competencia; los riesgos inherentes de operar un negocio global, incluidos los posibles efectos negativos derivados de la invasión rusa a Ucrania; los precios y disponibilidad de café, lácteos y otras materias primas; el efecto de los procedimientos legales; los efectos de los cambios en las leyes tributarias y las guías y regulaciones relacionadas que pueden implementarse y otros riesgos detallados en los documentos presentados por la Compañía ante la Comisión de Bolsa y Valores (Securities and Exchange Commission), incluso en los “Factores de riesgo” y “Discusión y análisis de la administración de la condición financiera y los resultados de Operaciones” de los informes periódicos presentados más recientemente por la Compañía en el Formulario 10-K y el Formulario 10-Q y presentaciones posteriores. La compañía no asume responsabilidad alguna por la actualización de estas declaraciones a futuro. El texto original en el idioma fuente de este comunicado es la versión oficial autorizada. Las traducciones solo se suministran como adaptación y deben cotejarse con el texto en el idioma fuente, que es la única versión del texto que tendrá un efecto legal.

title: Conselho de administração da Starbucks confirma os detalhes da transição do CEO
text: SEATTLE--(BUSINESS WIRE)--O conselho de administração da Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ: SBUX) forneceu hoje uma atualização para confirmar os detalhes da pesquisa para o próximo CEO da empresa. Conforme declarado durante a teleconferência de resultados trimestrais da empresa no mês passado, a busca do conselho continua no caminho certo para identificar um CEO sucessor nos próximos meses, e o conselho concordou com Schultz que ele permanecerá como CEO interino até o primeiro trimestre fiscal de 2023. Essa cronologia fornece à empresa a pista ideal para uma transição perfeita e continuidade da liderança durante a temporada de festas de 2022, à medida que a transformação dos negócios continua. Desde que voltou como CEO interino em 4 de abril, Schultz se envolveu profundamente com parceiros (funcionários) de todos os níveis da empresa, trabalhando diretamente com a equipe de liderança para moldar um plano estratégico para o futuro de uma Starbucks Coffee Company reimaginada. O plano de reinvenção está sendo projetado por meio da cocriação em toda a organização, com foco em superar as expectativas de parceiros e clientes. À medida que o próximo CEO for nomeado para o cargo, ele fará uma imersão total na empresa, colaborando diretamente com Schultz. Em direção a um anúncio neste outono, a entrega oficial ocorrerá no primeiro trimestre de 2023. Depois disso, Schultz permanecerá no conselho da Starbucks. Sobre a Starbucks Desde 1971, a Starbucks Coffee Company tem o compromisso com o abastecimento ético e a torrefação do café arábica de alta qualidade. Hoje, com mais de 34 mil lojas em todo o mundo, a empresa é hoje a mais importante varejista e torrefadora mundial de cafés especiais. Por meio de nosso sólido compromisso com a excelência e nossos princípios orientadores, damos vida à exclusiva Experiência Starbucks para cada um de nossos clientes em cada copo de café. Para compartilhar a experiência, visite-nos em nossas lojas ou on-line em stories.starbucks.com ou em www.starbucks.com. Declarações prospectivas Algumas declarações aqui contidas são declarações “prospectivas” com o significado conferido pelas leis e normas de títulos mobiliários aplicáveis. Em geral, essas declarações podem ser identificadas pelo uso de palavras tais como “buscar”, “antecipar”, “acreditar”, “continuar”, “poder”, “estimar”, “esperar”, “sentir”, “prever”, “pretender”, “poderia”, “perspectiva”, “planejar”, “potencial”, “projeto”, “procurar”, “deveria”, “vai”, “gostaria”, indicadores de futuro do presente e do pretérito, além de expressões semelhantes com a finalidade de identificar declarações prospectivas, apesar de nem todas as declarações prospectivas conterem essas palavras identificadoras. Essas declarações incluem declarações relacionadas a tendências ou expectativas relacionadas aos efeitos de nossas iniciativas, estratégias, investimentos e planos existentes e futuros, bem como tendências ou expectativas em relação aos nossos resultados financeiros e modelo e direcionadores de crescimento de longo prazo; nossas operações nos EUA e na China; nossos esforços ambientais, sociais e de governança; nossos parceiros; tendências econômicas e de consumo, incluindo o impacto das pressões inflacionárias; a conversão de diversas operações de mercado para modelos totalmente licenciados; nossos planos para agilizar nossas operações, incluindo aberturas, fechamentos e mudanças nos formatos e modelos de lojas; expandir nosso licenciamento para a Nestlé de nossos negócios de bens de consumo embalados e Foodservice e seus efeitos nos resultados do segmento de Desenvolvimento de Canais; taxas de imposto; oportunidades de negócios e expansão; aquisições estratégicas; nossos programas de dividendos; custos de commodities e nossa estratégia de mitigação; nossa liquidez, fluxo de caixa das operações, investimentos, capacidade de captação e uso de recursos; continuando o cumprimento de nossas avenças sob nossas linhas de crédito e programa de papel comercial; repatriação de dinheiro para os EUA; a probabilidade de emissão de dívida adicional e a taxa de juros aplicável; o impacto contínuo da pandemia da covid-19 em nossos resultados financeiros e a disponibilidade futura de subsídios governamentais para a covid-19 ou outros eventos de saúde pública; nossa transição de CEO; nosso programa de recompra de ações; nosso uso de caixa e necessidades de caixa; os efeitos esperados de novos pronunciamentos contábeis e o impacto estimado de mudanças na legislação tributária dos EUA, incluindo alíquotas de impostos, investimentos financiados por essas mudanças e resultados potenciais; e efeitos de processos judiciais. Tais declarações são baseadas em informações operacionais, financeiras e competitivas atualmente disponíveis e estão sujeitas a vários riscos e incertezas. Os resultados e tendências futuras reais podem diferir materialmente dependendo de vários fatores, incluindo, mas não se limitando a: maior disseminação da covid-19 e interrupções relacionadas aos nossos negócios; medidas regulatórias ou ações voluntárias que podem ser implementadas para limitar a propagação da covid-19, incluindo restrições às operações comerciais ou requisitos de distanciamento social, e a duração e eficácia de tais restrições; o ressurgimento de infecções por covid-19 e a circulação de novas variantes de covid-19; flutuações nas economias e moedas dos EUA e internacionais; nossa capacidade de preservar, crescer e alavancar nossas marcas; a capacidade de nossos parceiros de negócios e fornecedores terceirizados de cumprir suas responsabilidades e compromissos; potenciais efeitos negativos de incidentes envolvendo doenças transmitidas por alimentos ou bebidas, falsificação, adulteração, contaminação ou rotulagem incorreta; potenciais efeitos negativos de violações materiais de nossos sistemas de tecnologia da informação na medida em que experimentamos uma violação material; falhas materiais de nossos sistemas de tecnologia da informação; custos associados e a execução bem-sucedida das iniciativas e planos da Empresa; novas iniciativas e planos ou revisões de iniciativas ou planos existentes; nossa capacidade de obter financiamento em termos aceitáveis; a aceitação dos produtos da Empresa por nossos clientes, evolução das preferências e gostos dos consumidores e mudanças no comportamento de gastos do consumidor; investimentos de parceiros, mudanças na disponibilidade e custo de mão de obra, incluindo quaisquer esforços de organização sindical e nossas respostas a tais esforços; falha em atrair ou reter executivos-chave ou talentos de funcionários; aumento significativo dos custos logísticos; pressões inflacionárias; o impacto da concorrência; riscos inerentes de operar um negócio global, incluindo quaisquer potenciais efeitos negativos decorrentes da invasão russa da Ucrânia; os preços e disponibilidade de café, laticínios e outras matérias-primas; o efeito de processos judiciais; os efeitos das mudanças nas leis tributárias e orientações e regulamentos relacionados que podem ser implementados e outros riscos detalhados nos registros da Empresa junto à Comissão de Valores Mobiliários, incluindo os “Fatores de risco” e “Discussão da administração e análise da condição financeira e resultados de operações” dos relatórios periódicos arquivados mais recentemente da Empresa no Formulário 10-K e Formulário 10-Q e arquivamentos subsequentes. A empresa não assume obrigação de atualizar essas declarações prospectivas. O texto no idioma original deste anúncio é a versão oficial autorizada. As traduções são fornecidas apenas como uma facilidade e devem se referir ao texto no idioma original, que é a única versão do texto que tem efeito legal.

title: Starbucks cleans house, looks outside company for new CEO

title: Starbucks CEO Updates Put SBUX Stock in Focus

title: Saudi PIF shortlisted as bidder for stake in Starbucks Mideast - sources
text: Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund, the Public Investment Fund (PIF), has been shortlisted to buy a stake in the Middle East, North Africa and central Asia Starbucks franchise held by Alshaya Group, two sources told Reuters.

title: Starbucks is looking externally for its next CEO, Howard Schultz says
text: Starbucks' next CEO will come from outside the company, interim leader Howard Schultz told the Wall Street Journal.

title: Starbucks says Schultz to remain CEO while company looks for successor
text: Starbucks Corp. SBUX, -0.47% said Monday it is still on track with its search for a successor chief executive, as stated on its quarterly earnings conference call last month. The coffee shop chain said Howard Schultz has agreed to remain as interim CEO through the first fiscal quarter of 2023.

title: Starbucks' Schultz to remain interim CEO until Q1 2023
text: Starbucks Corp said on Monday Howard Schultz would remain the coffee chain's interim chief executive officer until the end of the first quarter next year, as it looks for a permanent successor.

title: Starbucks Board of Directors Affirms CEO Transition Details
text: SEATTLE--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ: SBUX) Board of Directors today provided an update to affirm the search details for the Company’s next chief executive officer. As stated during the Company’s quarterly earnings call last month, the Board’s search continues to be on track to identify a successor ceo in the coming months, and the Board has agreed with Schultz that he will remain as interim ceo through the first fiscal quarter of 2023. This timeline provides the company the ideal runway for a seamless transition and continuity of leadership through the 2022 holiday season, as the business transformation continues. Since returning as interim ceo on April 4, Schultz has engaged deeply with partners (employees) of all levels of the company, working directly with the leadership team to shape a strategic plan for the future of a reimagined Starbucks Coffee Company. The reinvention plan is being designed through co-creation across the organization with a focus on exceeding the expectations of both partners and customers. As the next chief executive is named to the position, he or she will undergo full immersion into the Company, collaborating with Schultz directly. Driving toward an announcement this Fall, the official handoff will take place in the first calendar quarter of 2023. Thereafter, Schultz will remain on the Starbucks Board. About Starbucks Since 1971, Starbucks Coffee Company has been committed to ethically sourcing and roasting high-quality arabica coffee. Today, with more than 34,000 stores worldwide, the company is the premier roaster and retailer of specialty coffee in the world. Through our unwavering commitment to excellence and our guiding principles, we bring the unique Starbucks Experience to life for every customer through every cup. To share in the experience, please visit us in our stores or online at stories.starbucks.com or www.starbucks.com. Forward-Looking Statements Certain statements contained herein are “forward-looking” statements within the meaning of the applicable securities laws and regulations. Generally, these statements can be identified by the use of words such as “aim,” “anticipate,” “believe,” “continue,” “could,” “estimate,” “expect,” “feel,” “forecast,” “intend,” “may,” “outlook,” “plan,” “potential,” “project,” “seek,” “should,” “will,” “would,” and similar expressions intended to identify forward-looking statements, although not all forward-looking statements contain these identifying words. These statements include statements relating to trends in or expectations relating to the effects of our existing and any future initiatives, strategies, investments and plans, as well as trends in or expectations regarding our financial results and long-term growth model and drivers; our operations in the U.S. and China; our environmental, social and governance efforts; our partners; economic and consumer trends, including the impact of inflationary pressures; the conversion of several market operations to fully licensed models; our plans for streamlining our operations, including store openings, closures and changes in store formats and models; expanding our licensing to Nestlé of our consumer packaged goods and Foodservice businesses and its effects on our Channel Development segment results; tax rates; business opportunities and expansion; strategic acquisitions; our dividends programs; commodity costs and our mitigation strategy; our liquidity, cash flow from operations, investments, borrowing capacity and use of proceeds; continuing compliance with our covenants under our credit facilities and commercial paper program; repatriation of cash to the U.S.; the likelihood of the issuance of additional debt and the applicable interest rate; the continuing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our financial results and future availability of governmental subsidies for COVID-19 or other public health events; our ceo transition; our share repurchase program; our use of cash and cash requirements; the expected effects of new accounting pronouncements and the estimated impact of changes in U.S. tax law, including on tax rates, investments funded by these changes and potential outcomes; and effects of legal proceedings. Such statements are based on currently available operating, financial and competitive information and are subject to various risks and uncertainties. Actual future results and trends may differ materially depending on a variety of factors, including, but not limited to: further spread of COVID-19 and related disruptions to our business; regulatory measures or voluntary actions that may be put in place to limit the spread of COVID-19, including restrictions on business operations or social distancing requirements, and the duration and efficacy of such restrictions; the resurgence of COVID-19 infections and the circulation of novel variants of COVID-19; fluctuations in U.S. and international economies and currencies; our ability to preserve, grow and leverage our brands; the ability of our business partners and third-party providers to fulfill their responsibilities and commitments; potential negative effects of incidents involving food or beverage-borne illnesses, tampering, adulteration, contamination or mislabeling; potential negative effects of material breaches of our information technology systems to the extent we experience a material breach; material failures of our information technology systems; costs associated with, and the successful execution of, the Company’s initiatives and plans; new initiatives and plans or revisions to existing initiatives or plans; our ability to obtain financing on acceptable terms; the acceptance of the Company’s products by our customers, evolving consumer preferences and tastes and changes in consumer spending behavior; partner investments, changes in the availability and cost of labor including any union organizing efforts and our responses to such efforts; failure to attract or retain key executive or employee talent; significant increased logistics costs; inflationary pressures; the impact of competition; inherent risks of operating a global business including any potential negative effects stemming from the Russian invasion of Ukraine; the prices and availability of coffee, dairy and other raw materials; the effect of legal proceedings; the effects of changes in tax laws and related guidance and regulations that may be implemented and other risks detailed in the Company’s filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including in the “Risk Factors” and “Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations” sections of the Company's most recently filed periodic reports on Form 10-K and Form 10-Q and subsequent filings. The company assumes no obligation to update any of these forward-looking statements.
